response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack web/backend engineer with deep experience in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role requires a Principal Performance Architect specializing in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows essentially no experience in chip architecture, SIMD/MIMD, GPU architecture, DNN training/inference optimization, or hardware modeling. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and a wave-field-synthesis thesis are the only adjacent signals, and they are far too light for a principal-level hardware performance role.
strengths: Long track record (12+ years) as a senior/staff software engineer across multiple stacks
Some low-level/performance exposure via DLang microservices handling millions of req/s at Sociomantic
Brief C/C++ experience at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or DNN training/inference optimization background
Educational and experiential profile (BS + web/full-stack career) does not match Principal hardware performance IC requirements

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No deep-learning workload or AI accelerator optimization background
Senior-level systems and performance-sensitive backend experience
